finding: Across autodoro, dotfiles, jhana, intender, and weekend repos, the user issues some variant of 'commit and push' or 'commit, reload, push' at the end of nearly every work session. The sequence is always the same: stage relevant files, write a commit message, push to remote. A post-task skill or a '/ship' slash command that commits all staged changes with an auto-generated message and pushes could eliminate this recurring manual step. The dotfiles repo even has a dedicated auto-sync agent, yet the user still triggers manual commits there.
existing_mitigation: The repo-autosync skill exists in ~/.claude/skills/ and is configured for the dotfiles repo, but the user still manually commits in other repos. No general-purpose 'commit and push' automation covers all projects.

finding: After editing autodoro code, the user repeatedly asks Claude to 'reload the service' or 'reload autodoro'. In session 8bb79e52, the user explicitly asked for a git post-push hook that reloads the service, but subsequent sessions show the reload is still done manually. A git post-commit or post-push hook in the autodoro repo that runs 'systemctl --user restart autodoro' (or equivalent) would make every code change immediately live without manual intervention.
existing_mitigation: The user requested a post-push hook in session 8bb79e52, and there is a mention of adding git hooks instructions to CLAUDE.md. However, logs show the manual reload pattern continues in later sessions, suggesting the hook was either not fully implemented or not persisted.

finding: Across several intender sessions, the user manually triggers Chrome extension builds for store submission: 'pack for store', 'build a new store asset for manual upload', 'run the capture screenshots/hero'. The repo already has screenshot generation scripts, but the full publish-ready workflow (build zip, generate screenshots, verify icons) is still assembled by hand each time. A single '/store-prep' command or Makefile target that runs the full pipeline (build, generate screenshots, validate manifest, output zip) could make store submissions one-step.
existing_mitigation: Screenshot generation scripts exist in the intender repo. CI/CD for Chrome Web Store upload was started but then commented out. No single command produces a complete store-ready package.

finding: Three separate artcraft sessions begin with the user asking 'build/run project', 'git pull then build', or 'how do I run the app'. Each time Claude has to rediscover the build system. A per-project CLAUDE.md entry documenting the build/run command, or a convention like a top-level Makefile with 'make run', would let Claude (or the user) bootstrap any session instantly. This pattern also appears in the jhana repo where the emulator and app build steps are rediscovered repeatedly.
existing_mitigation: No per-project CLAUDE.md with build instructions was found for artcraft. The jhana repo has a CLAUDE.md but it focuses on harness architecture rather than quick-start commands.
